target.title: fix(imessage): stage remote media before plugin dispatch
target.body:
## Summary

- Stage remote inbound media during dispatch before plugin hook and plugin-bound inbound-claim metadata is built.
- Mark successfully staged remote media as already staged so the later agent reply path does not restage the same attachment.
- Add regressions for SCP remote-cache rewriting, plugin-bound Codex claim metadata, and Codex `localImage` turn input.

## Linked context

Closes #91744.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: remote iMessage attachments that start as raw macOS Messages paths are staged before plugin-bound Codex inbound claims are constructed, so the Codex-visible media metadata uses the local remote-cache path instead of `/Users/.../Library/Messages/Attachments/...`.
- Real environment tested: fresh OpenClaw worktree `/Users/andy/openclaw-91744-imessage-codex-staged-path` based on live `upstream/main` `9a1f2022b127f27312eeb7f57e42d477bddf2c19`, using the actual dispatch source, message hook mapper, and Codex turn-input module from this patch.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` in the worktree to verify the dispatch ordering in source, build the plugin inbound claim event through the real hook mapper, and convert that event through the real Codex turn-input builder.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the runtime command:

```json
{
  "dispatchOrder": "stage_remote_media_for_dispatch before deriveInboundMessageHookContext",
  "claimMediaPath": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg",
  "claimMediaPaths": [
    "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
  ],
  "codexInput": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "what is this?",
      "text_elements": []
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
    },
    {
      "type": "localImage",
      "path": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
    }
  ],
  "rawMessagesPathVisible": false,
  "localImagePath": "/tmp/openclaw-proof/.openclaw/media/remote-cache/agent-main-imessage/photo.jpg"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the dispatch source now stages remote media before `deriveInboundMessageHookContext()`, the plugin inbound claim metadata carries the staged remote-cache path, and the final Codex turn input contains `localImage` entries for that staged path while `rawMessagesPathVisible` is `false`.
- What was not tested: a live remote Mac/iMessage conversation, because this environment does not have live remote Mac/iMessage credentials.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.media-policy.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/channel-inbound-roots.contract.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-turn-input.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

Regression coverage added:

- SCP remote staging success rewrites raw remote iMessage paths to the local remote-cache path.
- Dispatch stages remote iMessage media before plugin-bound Codex inbound claim metadata is built.
- Codex turn input uses the staged remote-cache path as `localImage`.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`
- Highest-risk area: remote media staging now runs earlier in dispatch for remote inbound media, before plugin hooks and plugin-bound claims.
- Mitigation: the new dispatch staging is gated on `MediaRemoteHost`, a session key, inbound media, and `!MediaStaged`; successful staging marks `MediaStaged` to preserve the single-stage contract for later agent processing. Existing path policy and size checks remain in `stageSandboxMedia()`.

## Current review state

- Next action: CI and ClawSweeper review.
- Waiting on: normal PR checks and durable ClawSweeper verdict.
- Bot/reviewer comments addressed: implements the issue review direction to stage before plugin-bound Codex claim metadata is derived.

AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: feat(discord): show subagent progress
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Discord users currently have little visible feedback when an assistant turn delegates work to tracked sub-agents. Long-running turns can look idle after the initial message even though background sub-agents are still running, and a sub-agent failure is not visible on the original Discord message.

This is especially confusing in Discord because the platform does not let the same bot/user add the same emoji reaction multiple times to one message, so a repeated “worker active” reaction cannot represent concurrent sub-agent activity.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds Discord-scoped, best-effort sub-agent progress feedback on the original inbound Discord message:

- active tracked sub-agents receive configurable ordinal reactions (`1️⃣` … `🔟` by default)
- successful completion removes that sub-agent’s ordinal reaction
- non-OK completion removes the ordinal and leaves a durable configurable failure marker (`🔴` by default)
- Discord typing is kept alive independently while tracked sub-agents remain active, then stops when all tracked work finishes or the safety TTL expires

The implementation threads the source Discord message id through native `sessions_spawn`, tracked ACP spawn, requester origin metadata, and the `subagent_spawned` / `subagent_ended` hook payloads so the Discord extension can target the correct parent message. Non-Discord callers and callers without a source message id keep existing behavior.

## User Impact

Discord users get immediate, lightweight progress feedback during delegated work without waiting for the final assistant reply:

- `1️⃣`, `2️⃣`, etc. show how many tracked sub-agents are currently active for the turn
- the ordinal disappears when that sub-agent completes successfully
- `🔴` remains if any tracked sub-agent ends with a non-OK outcome
- typing stays active while tracked sub-agents are still running

Operators can configure or disable this behavior with `channels.discord.subagentProgress`, including reaction progress, ordinal emoji, failure emoji, typing keepalive, interval, and max-duration TTL.

The feature is best-effort and Discord-only. Reaction or typing API failures are debug-logged and do not block spawn/completion flow.

## Implementation Notes

- Adds source/current message id propagation through delivery context and `sessions_spawn` tool context.
- Carries that id into native sub-agent spawn and tracked ACP registration.
- Extends requester origin / hook metadata for `subagent_spawned` and `subagent_ended`.
- Adds a Discord progress tracker keyed by account id, parent channel id, typing/thread target id, and source message id to avoid thread collisions.
- Reuses released ordinal slots and avoids duplicate reaction attempts when active concurrency exceeds the configured ordinal list.
- Keeps overflow runs in tracker state for active-count and typing cleanup even when no ordinal reaction can be assigned.
- Uses `unref` on typing timers where supported so keepalive intervals do not keep Node alive.
- Updates Discord config schema, UI hints, generated channel config metadata, and docs.

## Evidence

### Real Behavior Proof — focused automated coverage

Passed locally on branch `feature/discord-subagent-progress` at commit `abf892f76f2`:

```bash
pnpm test src/utils/delivery-context.test.ts \
  src/agents/sessions-spawn-hooks.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts
```

This verifies:

- source/current message id propagation through delivery context
- native `sessions_spawn` requester metadata
- tracked ACP spawned/ended hook metadata
- Discord progress disabled behavior
- active ordinal add/remove behavior
- failure marker behavior for non-OK completion
- per-thread tracker separation and typing target behavior
- ordinal overflow and released-slot reuse
- Discord config schema validation

Additional focused regression pass after final polish:

```bash
pnpm test src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/boundary-invariants.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/subagent-hooks.test.ts
```

This verifies the final hook metadata and bundled typed-hook registration guard changes.

### Real Behavior Proof — static/config/docs gates

Passed locally:

```bash
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm config:channels:check
pnpm docs:list
git diff --check
```

These cover core TypeScript correctness, generated Discord channel config metadata consistency, docs link/list validation, and whitespace/conflict hygiene.

### Real Behavior Proof — manual/live gap

A live Discord API scenario has not been run from this branch yet. The recommended final manual gate is a Discord-originated turn that spawns multiple tracked sub-agents, including one non-OK completion, from both a normal channel and a thread/forum context.

Expected live results:

- active ordinal reactions are added to the original Discord message
- ordinals are removed as successful sub-agents complete
- `🔴` remains after a failed/non-OK sub-agent
- typing stays active while tracked sub-agents are running
- typing stops after all tracked sub-agents finish or after the configured safety TTL

target.title: Add reset family carryover summaries
target.body:
## Behavior or issue addressed

Depends on #90239.

When a session has been reset/rotated, the active transcript can be clean while the previous reset archive still contains the latest compaction summary. The next embedded-agent prompt therefore starts without the carryover context unless the model/tool explicitly asks for family history.

This change reuses the session-family transcript resolver from #90239 and injects the latest reset-archive compaction summary into embedded-agent prompt context before the LLM boundary, but only when the active context does not already contain a compaction summary.

## Fix

- Move the #90239 family/reset transcript target resolver into a shared gateway helper.
- Keep `chat.history(includeFamily)` on the shared resolver.
- Pass the current session store path/entry into embedded-agent attempts.
- Add a prompt-context transform that prepends one `compactionSummary` from reset ancestor transcripts.
- Skip injection when the active session already has a compaction summary.
- Cap family transcript read targets before reading files; ancestor targets stop at the cap while current-session targets remain available.
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` and preserved the newer transcript-reader seam plus same-session reset fallback.

## Real behavior proof

**Current head proven:** `90525a0927bfcd94912dfa9c3d702c6de6cb231d`.

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A reset/rotated logical session should read bounded reset-family history through the shared resolver, keep current reset archive plus current active transcript available inside the cap, inject exactly one reset-family compaction summary before the embedded-agent LLM boundary, and cap oversized archived summaries before prompt injection.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `/home/clever/.openclaw/workspace/github/openclaw`, using current branch TypeScript modules and temporary on-disk session stores/transcripts under `/tmp/openclaw-family-proof-905-*`. No private channel, endpoint, phone, prompt, or auth data was used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
90525a0927bfcd94912dfa9c3d702c6de6cb231d

$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# The script imported these current-branch modules:
# - ./src/gateway/session-history-family.ts
# - ./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.ts
# It created a temporary sessions.json, 35 ancestor transcripts,
# one current reset archive transcript, and one current active transcript.
# Ancestor 29 contained an oversized compaction summary larger than
# CARRYOVER_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS. The script called the real family target
# resolver, real carryover resolver, installed the prompt transform,
# applied the transform twice, printed the observed result, and removed temp files.
TS
{
  "head": "90525a0927bfcd94912dfa9c3d702c6de6cb231d",
  "targetCount": 32,
  "firstTargets": [
    "ancestor-proof-00:ancestor",
    "ancestor-proof-01:ancestor",
    "ancestor-proof-02:ancestor"
  ],
  "lastTargets": [
    "ancestor-proof-29:ancestor",
    "current-proof-session:reset-archive",
    "current-proof-session:active"
  ],
  "currentResetTargetPresent": true,
  "currentActiveLast": true,
  "ancestor29Present": true,
  "ancestor30Present": false,
  "ancestor34Present": false,
  "carryoverSummaryLength": 16384,
  "carryoverSummaryMaxChars": 16384,
  "carryoverSummaryEndsTruncated": true,
  "carryoverSummaryPrefix": "Exact-head 905 oversized carryover 29: x",
  "carryoverTokensBefore": 2029,
  "promptRolesBeforeLlm": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ],
  "compactionSummaryCount": 1,
  "idempotentRoles": [
    "compactionSummary",
    "user"
  ]
}
```

**Evidence after fix:**

- The shared resolver returned 32 targets and kept both current reset archive and current active transcript inside the cap.
- The current active transcript was ordered last for final history trimming.
- Ancestors beyond the reserved cap were not read: `ancestor30Present` and `ancestor34Present` are false.
- Carryover selected the latest ancestor inside the capped family window, `ancestor-proof-29`, and preserved its token count.
- The oversized archived compaction summary was capped before prompt injection: `carryoverSummaryLength` equals `carryoverSummaryMaxChars` (16384) and `carryoverSummaryEndsTruncated` is true.
- The embedded prompt transform injected exactly one `compactionSummary` before the model boundary and remained idempotent on a second pass.

**Observed result after fix:** The terminal output showed `targetCount: 32`, current reset archive plus current active transcript retained at the end of the target list, `ancestor30Present: false`, `ancestor34Present: false`, one pre-LLM `compactionSummary`, idempotent second transform roles, and an oversized summary capped exactly at 16384 chars with `[summary truncated]`.

**Validation on current head:**

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  20 passed (20)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/session-family-carryover.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  8 passed (8)

$ git diff --check
# no output

$ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json
# passed
```

**What was not tested:** Full live Telegram/Slack/Discord end-to-end reset turn was not run locally; the proof exercises the real resolver and embedded carryover path against on-disk OpenClaw-like session artifacts.

target.title: fix(telegram): make pre-connect network failures recoverable in send context
target.body:
## Summary

grammy's Telegram bot library can emit bare `"Network request for 'X' failed!"` errors for pre-connect failures (DNS resolution, TLS handshake, etc.) — errors that are safe to retry since the request never reached Telegram's servers.

The existing regex `GRAMMY_NETWORK_REQUEST_FAILED_AFTER_RE` required the `"\s+after\b"` clause, so these bare pre-connect failures were not classified as recoverable in `context: send` and messages were dropped instead of retried.

## Fix

Relax the regex to make `"after"` optional:

```diff
- const GRAMMY_NETWORK_REQUEST_FAILED_AFTER_RE =
-   /^network request(?:\s+for\s+["']?[^"']+["']?)?\s+failed\s+after .*[!.]?$/i;
+ const GRAMMY_NETWORK_REQUEST_FAILED_AFTER_RE =
+   /^network request(?:\s+for\s+["']?[^"']+["']?)?\s+failed(?:\s+after .*)?[!.]?$/i;
```

| Message | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `Network request for 'sendMessage' failed after 2 attempts.` | ✅ recoverable | ✅ recoverable |
| `Network request for 'sendMessage' failed!` | ❌ dropped | ✅ recoverable |
| `Network request for 'X' failed!` | ❌ dropped | ✅ recoverable |

The existing test case covering `"failed after N attempts."` continues to pass — only the pre-connect failure path is corrected.

## Verification

- Regex behavior verified against known grammy error message patterns
- Existing `"failed after N attempts."` test case still passes (same behavior)
- TypeScript syntax check: clean
- 2 files changed, 5 insertions, 3 deletions

Closes #80362
target.author: kailigithub

target.title: perf(agents): reuse media tool availability during tool prep
target.body:
## Summary
- Reuse the optional media tool factory plan for image, video, and music generation tool registration.
- Let media generation tool factories skip duplicate registration-time availability scans when `createOpenClawTools` already resolved availability.
- Preserve latest main behavior for async media task yielding and explicit-workspace image provider planning.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Repeated warm `createOpenClawCodingTools` prep was spending about 29ms each registering `image_generate`, `video_generate`, and `music_generate` because those factories repeated availability checks already resolved by the OpenClaw media factory plan.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `media-tool-availability-pr4c`; original warm in-process tool prep probe after one warmup call, plus current-head regression verification after rebasing onto `origin/main` at `4dad7bd93b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local `node`/`tsx` probe that imports `createOpenClawCodingTools`, records `recordToolPrepStage` timings, discards one warmup call, then records 30 warm samples; after the latest rebase, ran the focused media-tool regression suite, core test typecheck, formatter check, `git diff --check`, and `pnpm build` on current head `e242dccc50`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
original warm prep probe after fix:
total p50 103.68ms, p95 110.16ms
openclaw-tools:image_generate registration p50 0.005ms
openclaw-tools:video_generate registration p50 0.002ms
openclaw-tools:music_generate registration p50 0.002ms
/usr/bin/time -l max RSS 850575360 bytes

current-head regression verification:
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot
4 test files passed, 178 tests passed

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.media-factory-plan.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
exit 0

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
exit 0

pnpm build
exit 0; phase timings total 140.7s, slowest tsdown 91.0s
```

Observed result after fix: Single-call warm p50 improved by about 84ms versus the same-probe baseline, from 187.74ms p50 to 103.68ms p50. The three generation registration stages dropped from about 29ms each to near-zero, while direct factory calls and execution-time provider resolution remain guarded by tests.

What was not tested: Full end-to-end paid provider media generation was not run; this PR changes registration-time availability reuse and preserves execution-time provider resolution paths.

target.title: fix(agents): handle MiniMax prompt_cache_hit_tokens in normalizeUsage
target.body:
MiniMax reports prompt_tokens (total, including cached tokens) and prompt_cache_hit_tokens (cache portion) as separate fields. Without handling prompt_cache_hit_tokens in normalizeUsage, the cache subtraction is skipped, causing derivePromptTokens to double-count cached tokens. This inflates the context token ratio and triggers premature compaction.

- Add prompt_cache_hit_tokens to UsageLike type
- Add prompt_cache_hit_tokens to cacheRead extraction chain
- Add prompt_cache_hit_tokens to usesOpenAIStylePromptTotals check
- Add test case for MiniMax normalization behavior

Fixes #68470

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

target.title: fix(gateway): preserve text from oversized multimodal history
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve recoverable text blocks from oversized multimodal transcript lines
- replace omitted large image payloads with a lightweight placeholder instead of dropping the full message
- add a regression test covering text + oversized image history replay

Fixes #78729

## Root cause
When a transcript JSONL line exceeded the oversized-line guard, the tail reader fell back to a generic placeholder record and replaced the entire message with `[chat.history omitted: message too large]`. That meant a large inline image/base64 payload could cause nearby normal text in the same multimodal message to disappear from `chat.history`.

## Testing
- `pnpm test -- src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts`

target.title: feat(browser): My Browser node-anchored turn routing (stacks on #93411)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #93411 — that must merge first.** Builds on the run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive from #93411; the diff currently overlaps #93411's commits and will be rebased to the extension-only delta once #93411 lands. Draft until then.

## Summary

Makes "My Browser" a usable side-panel copilot on top of node-anchored tool-scoping. Four blocks:

**1. Node-anchored turn routing (the original core).** Routes the side-panel turn **through the hosting node** as an authenticated node-originated `agent.request`, so the gateway confines that turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411), and pins the browser proxy back to that same node. Falls back to a direct gateway turn when no node hosts the bridge.
- Node-host event emitter (`node-event-emitter.ts`, re-exported via `plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime`); extension bridge forwards the side-panel `agent.request` via an `onAgentRequest` callback threaded from `server-lifecycle`; browser tool pins to the turn's `hostingNodeId`.

**2. Browser-copilot persona as a system prompt.** `server-lifecycle` injects `BROWSER_COPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as `extraSystemPrompt` on every node-originated side-panel `agent.request` (server-side, not client-injectable). Without it the agent tends to refuse browser tasks / fetch over HTTP; with it, it acts through the browser tool, defers only CAPTCHA/2FA/payment, and won't reload a page it's already on.

**3. Side-panel UX (`sidepanel.js`/`background.js`).**
- **Per-tab agent sessions** — deterministic key per pinned tab (`…:thread:tab-<id>`), so each attached tab has its own resumable conversation; "+ New session" resets the thread.
- **Live tab-context injection** — each turn carries the pinned tab's current URL+title, so the agent knows where it is without a tool round-trip and won't re-navigate a page it's on.
- **Streaming render fixes** — render the gateway's authoritative full text (no duplicate/garbled deltas), render line breaks, and split inter-tool commentary into separate persisted bubbles with a `→ <tool>` step line per tool call (the panel opts into `tool-events`).

**4. Bridge reliability.**
- **Page-only target announce** — only real `page` targets are announced to Playwright; worker/`blob:`/service-worker/cross-origin-iframe child targets are dropped (they were announced as phantom pages, hanging the next snapshot ~47s on heavy pages like signup flows).
- **Per-turn CDP keepalive** — reuse the cached CDP connection within an idle TTL, force a fresh reconnect after a gap; skip the per-op throwaway health-probe when a live connection is cached.
- **Idempotent `attachTab` + `getTargetId`** — re-attaching an already-connected tab no longer crashes Playwright with a Duplicate-target error; the panel can resolve a tab's CDP targetId to pin turns to it.

Out of scope: the `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive itself (#93411). The node-bridge driver uses its own literal `extension-bridge`, distinct from the legacy `extension` → `existing-session` value (whose doctor migration is preserved), so there is no deprecation collision.

## Linked context

Depends on #93411 (gateway-core `byNode`). Related #93347 (closed — superseded by the authenticated-node approach).

## Review findings addressed (local ClawSweeper / Codex review, head 11b72146a8)

Local + public ClawSweeper / Codex review rounds flagged the items below; all are resolved or explained:

1. **[HIGH] Unauthenticated extension bridge** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed.** When `gateway.auth.token` is configured, the `/extension` relay handshake must present a matching HMAC on `?token=` (recomputed server-side via WebCrypto, mirroring the extension's `deriveRelayToken`) or the socket is rejected (close 1008); tokenless loopback stays trusted-local. Proven live on the deployed build — `no-token → 1008`, `wrong-token → 1008`, `correct-HMAC → accepted` — plus 4 unit tests (`extension-bridge-server.auth.test.ts`).
2. **[MED] Hard-coded `DEFAULT_TOKEN`** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed.** Removed `test-token-for-container`; tokenless loopback now omits auth instead of sending a placeholder credential.
3. **[high] node-event emission on the public SDK** (`plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts`) — **fixed** (`04d6d08b3b`). `emitNodeGatewayEvent` (and the install primitives) are removed from the public plugin-SDK barrel and moved to a dedicated **private** entrypoint `node-gateway-events-internal` (mirroring `ssrf-runtime-internal`): built for the bundled first-party bridge but excluded from public package exports, so a third-party plugin cannot install an emitter or forge node-attributed events. The extension package-boundary checks (`no-plugin-sdk-internal`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`, `subpaths-exported`) all pass. (The private-subpath form as first implemented broke **runtime** resolution — found via live deploy, fixed in `b3e840c785`; see #14.)
4. **[P1] macOS toggle writes invalid config** (`ConfigStore.swift`) — **fixed.** Added `browser.priorDefaultProfile` to the config schema so the My Browser toggle's state write validates against the strict `browser` object.
5. **[P1] `driver:"extension"` collided with the legacy migration** — **fixed** (`677c875a8e`). The branch had repurposed the legacy `driver:"extension"` literal (which the doctor migrates to `existing-session`, Chrome MCP attach) for the new node-bridge mode *and* deleted that migration, so the doctor would silently rewrite a new-mode profile and legacy upgrades broke. Fixed by giving the new mode its own literal **`extension-bridge`** (zod union + refine, public type, the input-side resolver, the macOS toggle) and **restoring** the deleted `extension → existing-session` profile migration. `doctor-legacy-config` migration test passes; build clean. (The rename initially left the relay gate on the dead `extension` literal — found via live deploy, fixed in `f4f56efa2e`; see #15.)
6. **[P1] Dependency-graph change** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`) — **stacked-base drift; rebase/approval-gated.** The bundled `chrome-extension/package.json` is `private` and adds **no runtime dependencies**; the only lockfile changes are upstream override bumps (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `hono`, `aws`, `acp`) carried by the #93411-stacked base, not this PR. They disappear on the rebase to the extension-only delta. Full resolution needs that rebase or maintainer dependency-guard approval — neither available pre-#93411.
7. **[P2] `config.example.json` relayPort** — **fixed** (`debaa45e3e`). Was `18790`; corrected to `18792` (`DEFAULT_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT`) so the bundled example dials the right bridge port.
8. **[P2] relay URL throws under tokenless auth** (`background-utils.js`) — **fixed** (`e1d56a8aba`). `buildRelayWsUrl` threw on a blank gateway token, but `gateway.auth.mode:"none"` is valid; it now returns a tokenless `/extension` URL (the node bridge accepts it only when it also has no configured token, so a token-protected bridge still rejects it).
9. **[P1] Schema-invalid macOS profile** (`ConfigStore.swift:178`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). The My Browser toggle wrote `{driver:"extension-bridge"}` but the zod profile schema requires `color`; the toggle now writes a `color` so the saved config validates. (Distinct from #4, which added `priorDefaultProfile`.)
10. **[P1] Copilot prompt dropped** (`server-node-events.ts`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `server-lifecycle` emits `extraSystemPrompt` on the node-originated `agent.request`, but the receiver's `AgentDeepLink` parser omitted the field, so the browser-copilot persona never reached the agent. Added `extraSystemPrompt` to the parsed type + the `agentCommandFromIngress` dispatch input (which already threads it into the run).
11. **[P1] Explicit relay host pointed at the gateway** (`background.js getRelayHost`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `getRelayHost` returned the gateway URL's hostname, but the node-owned CDP bridge is a *local loopback* intake (it drives the user's local Chrome); with a remote gateway (per `config.example`'s `wss://…ts.net`) the relay would dial the wrong host. It now defaults to `127.0.0.1`, with an explicit `relayHost` override for advanced setups.
12. **[high] Node-routing fail-open** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). A failed node turn silently fell back to a direct (unconfined) `sessions.send`, dropping `gateway.tools.byNode` confinement. The relay now records the intake's `nodeIntegrated` (via `/whoami`); `nodeTurn` returns `fallbackAllowed`; the panel **fails closed** (asks to reconnect the node) when a node was hosting — genuine gateway-only deployments still fall back.
13. **[P2] Options page blocked tokenless loopback** (`options.js`) — **fixed** (`c99fd6ab78`). The Options page hard-required a token, mismatching the relay/side-panel tokenless-loopback support (#8); it now allows an empty token for loopback gateways (`127.0.0.1`/`localhost`/`::1`) while still requiring one for remote gateways.
14. **[P0] F3 seam broke runtime module resolution — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`b3e840c785`). The F3 fix (#3) registered `node-gateway-events-internal` in `plugin-sdk-private-local-only-subpaths.json` — the **test/QA-exclusion bucket** (`ssrf-runtime-internal`, `qa-*`, `*-test-contracts`) that is omitted from the package `exports` map. In any **installed** package the bridge's runtime `import` threw `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`, so `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never bound the relay (the error was swallowed by its own `try/catch`) and node-anchored My Browser silently failed. Build, all three boundary checks, and static review passed — the break is runtime-only (the exclusion bucket is also exempt from the subpaths-exported check). Fixed in two steps: first made it a resolvable entrypoint (`b3e840c785`), then **refactored to a true private seam** (`baee5d38a8`) — the node-host injects `emitNodeGatewayEvent` into the bridge in-process via the `onNodeHostStart` hook, so it is **never on the public plugin-SDK surface** (a third-party plugin cannot import it; the surface/api budgets that flagged the exported form now pass for it) and it still throws without a registered node. `emitNodeGatewayEvent` stays removed from the public `gateway-runtime` barrel (the original finding).
15. **[P0] F4 rename left the relay gate on a dead literal — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`f4f56efa2e`). `ResolvedBrowserConfig.profiles` is `Record<string, BrowserProfileConfig>` — it holds the **raw input** driver, not the mapped resolved driver. So after the `extension → extension-bridge` rename (#5), the relay gate `needsBridge` (`server-lifecycle.ts:39`) compared a raw profile against the now-dead literal `"extension"` and was **always false** — the node-owned CDP bridge never bound, even with #14 fixed. Gate on the input `"extension-bridge"`. (`config.ts:552` and `profile-capabilities.ts:27` are correct — they operate on the mapped/resolved driver.) Verified live: the relay binds on the node-host **eager-start with no kick**, the extension reconnects, and the tab attaches.
16. **[high] Node-turn status failure could bypass confinement** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`12b9e03fa1`). The `catch` around the `nodeTurn` message treated a *rejected* `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` (background worker/relay unreachable) the same as a gateway-only deployment and fell through to a direct, unconfined `sessions.send`. The direct fallback now defaults **closed** and is allowed only on a positive gateway-only signal (`fallbackAllowed === true`); a node-hosted intake (`fallbackAllowed === false`) or a rejected message both fail closed, so a node-confined turn never silently drops `gateway.tools.byNode`.
17. **[high] node identity not surfaced on the bridge `/whoami`** (`server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`1ce3dccc87`). `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never passed an identity to `ensureExtensionBridge`, so `/whoami` always reported `nodeIntegrated:false`; the extension then treated a node-hosted bridge as gateway-only and allowed the fallback above. The hosting node's identity is now threaded through the DI seam (`runner → onNodeHostStart ctx → registry → ensureExtensionBridge`); verified live: `/whoami` returns `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` (+ `extension-bridge-server.whoami.test.ts`).
18. **[P1] browser CLI status didn't surface the `extension-bridge` driver** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`, refined `fc4fac3a29`). The profile status now surfaces the configured `extension-bridge` driver name (`server-context.ts` reverse-maps the resolved `extension` 1:1; `ProfileStatus.driver` type updated). An initial pass also widened the CLI *create-profile* input to advertise `extension-bridge`/`clawd`, but the create route only accepts `existing-session`, so that advertisement was reverted on review (`fc4fac3a29`) — those profiles are config/toggle-created, not CLI-created. The resolved→public mapping was then extracted into a documented, unit-tested pure function `toPublicProfileDriver` (`31440f233f`); its JSDoc records that a resolved `extension` only ever originates from a configured `extension-bridge` (legacy input `extension` is migrated to `existing-session` by the doctor *before* resolution), so the status stays consistent with the legacy `driver:"extension"` doctor migration with no collision. The same helper was then applied to the second public surface — the `browser status` route + `BrowserStatus` type (`1ec244274e`) — and a sweep of `browser/` confirmed the only remaining raw `extension` driver types are the internal `ResolvedBrowserProfile` + the helper param (correct).
19. **[P2] `extension-bridge` not documented in generated config help** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`). The zod `driver` union had no `.describe()`, so the generated config help omitted the driver values; added a description covering `openclaw`/`clawd`/`existing-session`/`extension-bridge`. (Build + config-help-quality test + browser-tool tests green.)
20. **[P1] node→gateway event emitter not re-registered after reconnect** (`runner.ts`) — **fixed** (`fc4fac3a29`). `onClose` nulls the emitter on disconnect (so plugins fail loudly rather than dropping a turn), but it was never re-registered, so a node-originated browser turn after a transient gateway reconnect stranded on a null emitter. Re-registered in `onHelloOk`, which fires on every (re)connect once the hello handshake completes.
21. **[medium] unauthenticated loopback CDP bridge face** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **hardened** (`b3533a10c5`). The `/extension` relay was HMAC-authed, but the loopback CDP face (`/devtools/browser/…`) Playwright connects to was open — a stray local process could discover targets and drive the user's real Chrome. The bridge now registers its token in the bridge-auth registry and requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the `/devtools/browser/` upgrade (constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual`); with no token configured it stays trusted-local (back-compat). `getHeadersWithAuth` presents the registered token so the node's `connectOverCDP` authenticates to its own bridge (non-bridge ports are unaffected); `/json` discovery stays open since the per-startup browser guid is the secret. Covered by `extension-bridge-server.cdp-auth.test.ts` and **verified E2E on a live node-anchored deploy — the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org with the CDP face now authenticated (no regression):**

![CDP-face auth E2E — the LLM agent drives the attached tab to example.org ("Done — the tab is now on https://www.example.org/") with the loopback CDP face now requiring the bridge token](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/cdp-auth-e2e.png)
22. **[high] node-gateway emitter exposed on the public plugin SDK** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`0913003ee2`, finalized `efa5cbd4c4`). The F3 DI seam put `emitNodeGatewayEvent` on the public `OpenClawPluginNodeHostCommand.onNodeHostStart` ctx, so any plugin author could originate node-attributed gateway events — the private boundary F3 was meant to keep. The emitter is removed from the public `onNodeHostStart` ctx type (which now carries only `nodeId`); the node-host passes it on the **runtime** ctx **only to the bundled node-anchored browser bridge**, gated on the **registry plugin identity** (`entry.pluginId === "browser"`, the bundled plugin's manifest id — not a forgeable self-declared cap; `9a82c1d790`) — every other plugin's startup hook gets just `nodeId`; the emitter stays off the public `{nodeId?}` ctx type (the browser plugin reads it via an internal cast). (Three interim passes — a host-internal `plugin-sdk` subpath, an ungated runtime ctx, then a cap-gated ctx — were superseded to drop a package self-resolution, stop the emitter being visible to all plugin hooks, and gate on identity rather than a declarable cap.) Build + lint + tests green; **verified E2E across deploys** — bridge up, `nodeIntegrated:true`, the gated emitter still reaches the bridge which originates node-attributed turns (the agent drove the attached tab to example.net → example.com → iana.org).
23. **[P2] stale byNode tests (run-scoped contract)** — **fixed** (`b58b000d71`). The byNode tests (from the stacked #93411 base) targeted the retired session-global registry (`setSessionHostingNodeId`) instead of the run-scoped `hostingNodeId`, so they failed against the final implementation: byNode is enforced in `createOpenClawCodingTools` (`agent-tools.ts:932`, `resolveNodeScopedToolPolicy(options.hostingNodeId)`) where `hostingNodeId` is threaded through the run (`attempt.ts → params → agent-tools`); `resolveGatewayScopedTools` is a byNode no-op for non-node MCP/HTTP callers. The embedded test now passes `hostingNodeId` (where byNode bites); the gateway-resolver test is rewritten to a single no-op assertion. Both pass (68/68 + green). *(These are #93411 stacked-base test files — fixed here per request; they reconcile on the #93411 rebase.)*
24. **[medium] broad Chrome extension control surface** (`extensions/chrome-extension/manifest.json`) — **audited; manifest reduced to 10 genuinely-used permissions.** History: an initial narrowing (`4a75987af5`) wrongly removed `declarativeNetRequest` + `clipboardWrite` on a **faulty grep** (escaped `\|` matched a literal pipe → false 0 uses), reverted in `5effcef169`. On proper re-audit: `clipboardWrite` is used (`sidepanel.js:696` writeText) and stays; `declarativeNetRequest` backed **only the dead frame-strip rule**, removed in `3b2374b616` (see finding 26). The remaining 10 are all exercised — `debugger` (CDP, 13), `tabs` (22), `storage` (23), `webNavigation.onCompleted`, `sidePanel` (6), `tabGroups` (3), `alarms` (3), `scripting`, `activeTab`, `clipboardWrite`. Host scope required (bridge WebSockets → loopback/`*.ts.net`; `webNavigation` needs any-URL visibility). No over-broad/unused permission remains; the residual concern is product/security acceptance of CDP-based browser control, not a code change.
25. **[high] emitter gate could be shadowed by plugin id** (`src/node-host/plugin-node-host.ts`) — **fixed** (`5effcef169`). Gating the privileged node→gateway emitter on `entry.pluginId === "browser"` alone let a **config-loaded plugin shadow the bundled `"browser"` id** and receive it. Now gated on **both** the registry plugin's trusted bundled origin **and** id: `entry.origin === "bundled" && entry.pluginId === "browser"` — a config/external plugin is `origin !== "bundled"`, so it is **fail-safe denied**. Threaded the plugin record's trust `origin` (`PluginOrigin`) onto the `PluginNodeHostCommandRegistration` entry (type + the single `registry.ts` push site); the field is optional so non-registry fixtures default to `undefined` (denied). `tsgo:core` clean; `loader` (160) + `plugin-node-host` (2) tests pass; oxlint 0/0. **Re-deployed + E2E re-proven on the hardened gate:** the bundled browser plugin (`origin:"bundled"`) still passes — `node.invoke 2520ms` + `live session turn` 9.9s, agent drove the attached tab to iana.org:

![Emitter origin-gate E2E — after hardening the gate to require origin:"bundled" AND id:"browser", the bundled browser bridge still receives the emitter and the agent drives the attached tab to iana.org (a config plugin shadowing the id would be denied)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-gate-e2e.png)
26. **[high] frame-strip rule strips CSP/X-Frame-Options for no consumer** (`extensions/chrome-extension/background.js`) — **fixed** (`3b2374b616`). The `declarativeNetRequest` dynamic rule removed `X-Frame-Options` + `Content-Security-Policy` response headers for the gateway hostname in `sub_frame`s, per its comment *"so the gateway Control UI loads in the side panel iframe"* — but there is **no `<iframe>` anywhere** in the extension (`sidepanel.html`/`options.html` have none). Dead code that needlessly weakened header security. Removed the rule (`updateFrameStrippingRule` + its `gatewayUrl`-change listener + startup call + `FRAME_STRIP_RULE_ID`) and the now-unused `declarativeNetRequest` permission — eliminating the header-stripping entirely. `getGatewayUrl` stays (used elsewhere); extension oxlint delta 0. **Re-deployed + E2E re-proven:** 10 permissions, no DNR, agent drove the tab to example.org (`node.invoke 225ms` + `live session turn` 7.5s):

![Frame-strip-removal E2E — after deleting the dead frame-strip DNR rule and the declarativeNetRequest permission (10 permissions, no CSP/X-Frame-Options stripping), the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-no-framestrip-e2e.png)

**Disposition of the remaining (non-code) review items** — all investigated; actioned where a code action exists:
- **Rebase / "clean rebased diff after #93411"** — genuinely blocked, not deferred-by-choice: prerequisite **#93411 is still OPEN**, so its `byNode` primitive is not in `main`. Rebasing onto `main` now would *delete* the primitive #93680 depends on. Actionable only once #93411 merges.
- **Dependency Guard / "remove the dependency graph change"** — **#93680 adds zero npm dependencies.** `extensions/chrome-extension/package.json` declares no `dependencies`/`devDependencies` (only a static-asset build config), so its entire lockfile footprint is one empty importer entry (`extensions/chrome-extension: {}`). The version churn the guard reports is the stacked-base / branch-divergence delta, which resolves on the post-#93411 rebase — there is no #93680-introduced dependency to remove.
- **Config/default surfaces upgrade behavior** — rechecked, all additive/opt-in with no existing-install impact: `browser.priorDefaultProfile` is `z.string().optional()` (undefined on existing installs); `driver:"extension-bridge"` is a *new* schema literal with the default unchanged at `openclaw` and no legacy config carrying it (so no doctor migration is needed); `gateway.tools.byNode` is additive (inert unless a node is configured).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** a usable node-anchored browser copilot — node-confined turns, a persona that actually drives the browser, per-tab sessions, and a bridge that survives heavy real-world pages.
- **Real environment tested:** an OpenClaw gateway built from this branch driving the bundled extension in a headless Chromium (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) container, plus a device-paired loopback node for the byNode path, plus the standalone bridge + browser-tool unit suites.
- **Evidence after fix (live):**
  - `pnpm build` clean (`BUILD_EXIT=0`).
  - **byNode narrowing** a real node turn: `[byNode] allow=[web_search,image] before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (run-scoped via threaded `hostingNodeId`).
  - **Heavy-page (page-only filter):** navigating to `account.proton.me/mail/signup` — before, the snapshot timed out at ~47s on ~14 phantom targets; after, `skipped-non-page=2, timeouts=0` and the agent read the live form ("one field: Password").
  - **Tab-context:** asked the current URL with no tools, the agent answered `https://example.org/ — "Example Domain"` from injected context with `0` navigations (no reload).
  - **Per-tab isolation:** tab A told a codeword → tab B asked for it → "I don't have a codeword from you in this conversation."
  - **CDP keepalive:** per-op connect churn dropped 7→2 across a multi-step turn.
  - **FULL node-hop GUI flow (captured):** a paired loopback node hosting the extension bridge + the gateway with `nodes.browser.node`/`byNode`. A side-panel turn drove Chrome to IANA entirely through the node: gateway log shows the turn attributed to the node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`) and the browser tool proxied to it (`node.invoke ✓` ×3 → `browser.proxy`), the agent turn succeeded (`live session turn durationMs=27273`), and the tab rendered `iana.org` ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority"). Screenshot: the panel header reads "Connected — Internet Assigned N…" with the IANA page driven via the node.
  - **Relay auth gate (F1) — proven live on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** with `gateway.auth.token` configured, a WebSocket to `:18792/extension` is rejected (`close 1008`) with no token and with a wrong token, and accepted (server emits `connect.challenge`) only with the correct `deriveRelayToken` HMAC. Backed by 4 unit tests.
  - **Bootstrap-deadlock fix — re-proven on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** after killing the node-host (`:18792` → unbound) and restarting it with **no manual relay kick**, the node binds `:18792` at startup and the bridge answers `/json/version`.

**Verbatim run logs (latest build, redacted):**

```
# F1 relay-auth gate (live, gateway token configured)
no-token        => REJECTED (close 1008)
wrong-token     => REJECTED (close 1008)
correct-HMAC    => ACCEPTED (connect.challenge)

# Bootstrap-deadlock fix (node-host restart, NO manual relay kick)
:18792 after kill = 000
{"Browser":"Chrome/OpenClaw-Bridge","Protocol-Version":"1.3","webSocketDebuggerUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:18792/devtools/browser/<id>"}

# Node-hop turn (side-panel -> node -> attached Chrome tab)
[gateway] agent ... node=ffc1b7d3...        # turn attributed to the hosting node
node.invoke OK x3 (browser.proxy)           # browser tool proxied to that node
live session turn durationMs=27273          # byNode-scoped agent turn succeeded
tab -> iana.org ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority")

# F3 host-internal seam (extension package-boundary checks)
no-plugin-sdk-internal       : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
no-src-outside-plugin-sdk    : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
subpaths-exported            : OK: all referenced openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports are exported.
```

**Proof artifacts:** the full redacted run logs above are also collected in [this gist](https://gist.github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/17fba6011e351775e97d8b21a0d67ab7). Inspectable screenshot of a side-panel turn routed through the hosting node, driving the attached Chrome tab to IANA (panel header "Connected — Internet Assigned N…"):

![My Browser side panel — a turn routed through the hosting node drives the attached Chrome tab to iana.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/node_e2e2.png)

**Latest build (after the F3+F4 P0 fixes) — the LLM agent drives the user's real attached tab end-to-end:** on a clean install the relay binds on the node-host eager-start (no kick), the bundled extension reconnects and attaches the live tab, and side-panel turns are handled by the agent itself — routed through the hosting node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`, `live session turn durationMs=7211`), the agent calls its browser tool on the attached tab and replies in the panel. Below: a multi-turn session navigates the live tab `wikipedia.org → github.com` from side-panel turns ("Go to wikipedia.org" → "Now take me to github.com"), each confirmed by the agent (the panel shows "You're now on github.com.").

![My Browser — the LLM agent navigates the user's attached Chrome tab to github.com from a side-panel turn, routed through the hosting node](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-agent.gif)

**Comprehensive re-verification (recorded on `1ce3dccc87`, after the node-identity fix; the only later commits (`12b9e03fa1` fail-closed hardening, `c03c0c3585` driver surfacing, `fc4fac3a29` reconnect emitter re-registration + create-input revert, `31440f233f` driver-mapping extract/test, `1ec244274e` BrowserStatus mapping, `b3533a10c5` CDP-face auth, `0913003ee2`/`efa5cbd4c4`/`fe25b016f8`/`9a82c1d790` emitter off public SDK, identity-gated to the browser bridge; `b58b000d71` byNode test repair; `4a75987af5`→`5effcef169` manifest re-audit + emitter origin-gate, E2E re-proven on iana.org through the hardened gate; `3b2374b616` dead frame-strip rule + DNR removed, E2E re-proven on example.org) do not change these successful-turn behaviors):** `/whoami` now reports `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` on the node-hosted bridge, so the D-fix fail-closed actually fires.

**🎥 Live video on the latest-head runtime (after the full 12-round review chain, identity-gated emitter + run-scoped byNode + CDP-face auth all live):** the LLM agent drives the attached Chrome tab from a side-panel turn — typed prompt → `→ browser` → the tab navigates live → *"Done — now on https://www.wikipedia.org/"*:

![Live video — the LLM agent drives the attached Chrome tab to wikipedia.org from a side-panel turn, on the latest-head runtime (node-routed, identity-gated emitter)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-latest-head.gif)

A single multi-tab run shows **per-tab session isolation** — a codeword set in one tab's session is absent from another tab's session (Tab B's agent: *"You didn't give me any codeword in this chat … No codeword was shared"*, while MELON was set in Tab A):

![per-tab session isolation — the agent in the second tab's session has no knowledge of the first tab's codeword](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/per-tab-isolation.png)

![motion — codeword set in Tab A → switch to Tab B → agent there has no codeword → agent then drives Tab B](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-pertab.gif)

Each side-panel turn routes through the node on this head (`node.invoke ✓` browser.proxy + `claude live session turn` per turn). **byNode** confines a node turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode = {…ffc1b7d3…: {allow:[browser,web_search]}}`; the before→after narrowing (`[byNode] … before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{…}`, above) is captured against the **native agent runtime**. Honest note: the live test box runs the **claude-cli** backend, which sources its tools via MCP rather than the gateway's native tool resolver, so I could not re-surface that before→after on this box — the native-runtime capture stands, and byNode coverage of claude-cli backends is a `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411-primitive) question, not this PR's routing change.

> **Two P0 runtime regressions found via this live deploy** (findings 14–15): the F3 host-internal seam and the F4 driver rename each broke the relay at runtime while passing every static gate (build, the three boundary lints, code review, and the public review bot). A real-behavior deploy on a clean install is what surfaced them.

- **Observed result:** the node-routed turn is node-attributed and byNode-scoped; the persona-driven agent navigates and reads the live tab via the node; heavy pages snapshot instead of timing out; commentary renders as distinct steps; per-tab sessions stay isolated.
- **Bug found + fixed (node bootstrap deadlock):** the node-host **never eagerly started** the extension bridge — it bound `:18792` only lazily inside a `browser.proxy` command, which only originates from an agent turn, which needs the bridge up. Circular → node-anchored browser never worked out of the box. Confirmed: container LISTEN ports were `5900,7799,18789` until a `browser.proxy` was forced through the gateway, after which `:18792` bound and the full hop closed. **Fixed in this PR:** an optional `onNodeHostStart` hook on node-host commands; `runNodeHost` runs registered hooks once after the node→gateway emitter is wired; the browser plugin registers a hook that eager-starts the control service (no-op unless a profile uses `driver:"extension-bridge"`), via a lazy import so core never statically imports the extension. **Validated:** with the fix the node-host binds `:18792` at startup with no kick (`startBrowserControlServiceFromConfig` returns a live state and the bridge answers `/json/version`). `pnpm build` clean. (The captured GUI run pre-dated the build and used a one-time manual kick; the fix removes that need.)
- **What was not tested (honest gap):** the byNode *narrowing* log line is debug-level and not surfaced in this run; node-attribution + `node.invoke` proxying prove the hop, and `byNode` is configured + active. No remaining flow is unproven.
- **Proof limitations / environment constraints:** gateway + node ran as two processes on one host (loopback pairing) in the `oc-nodeanchor` container (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) — the earlier "no VM / disk-blocked" claim is retracted.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` clean.
- `extension-bridge-server.agent-request.test.ts` — forwards `agent.request` → `onAgentRequest`, error when no routing, empty-message reject, emitter-failure ack (4/4).
- `browser-tool.test.ts` — pins to the hosting node, explicit `node=` overrides (browser suite passing, no regressions).
- Container GUI behavioral runs (above) for persona / per-tab / tab-context / page-only filter / CDP keepalive.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — side panel routes through the node when present (direct-gateway fallback); per-tab sessions; injected tab context; new streaming render + tool-step lines.
- **Config/environment/migration change?** Uses #93411's `gateway.tools.byNode`; no new config.
- **Security/auth/tool-execution change?** Yes — turns become node-attributed so byNode can confine them; browser proxy pinned to the hosting node; persona is injected server-side (not client-supplied). Restriction-only via #93411.
- **Highest-risk areas + mitigations:**
  - *Node origination + reply delivery* → bridge only acks; replies use the existing gateway subscription; direct-turn fallback when no node.
  - *Page-only target filter* → only `page` targets announced; same-origin iframes still reached via the page's frame tree, so nothing is lost.
  - *CDP keepalive* → idle-TTL reconnect + the op self-heals if a cached socket is dead.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** hold for #93411 to merge, then rebase to the extension-only delta (which also drops the unrelated stacked-base lockfile drift).
- **Waiting on:** #93411 merge.
- **Addressed:** node-routing + browser-pin + persona + side-panel UX + bridge reliability wired, built clean; bootstrap-deadlock fixed; all six local-review findings resolved or explained (relay-auth gate + 4 unit tests, token hygiene, narrowed SDK surface, config schema; two non-issues explained); combined node-hop flow captured; **two P0 runtime regressions (F3 export resolution, F4 relay gate) found via live deploy and fixed** (`b3e840c785`, `f4f56efa2e`); on the fixed build the relay binds on the node-host eager-start with **no kick**, the bundled extension reconnects + attaches, and the **LLM agent drives the user's attached tab from side-panel turns** (`wikipedia.org → github.com`, recording embedded). F3's final form is the in-process DI seam (`baee5d38a8`).

commands.author: anyech

target.title: Guard Skill Workshop update applies against delta drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Skill Workshop update-apply footgun where an update proposal that contains a delta/proposal note can be applied as the full replacement `SKILL.md`.

For update proposals, `applySkillProposal()` strips proposal frontmatter and writes the proposal body as the live skill content. That is correct when the proposal body is a full updated skill, but unsafe when the body is an update note such as `# Update Proposal ...` with "proposed changes" instructions. In that case apply can replace the existing skill workflow with a proposal stub.

This PR adds an apply-time guard for update proposals before the live write, while still allowing intentional complete rewrites of a skill body.

## What Problem This Solves

Skill Workshop `action=update` proposals are meant to carry a complete replacement `SKILL.md`. Before this guard, apply-time code could accept a proposal/delta note and write that note as the live skill body. That risks destroying the actual skill procedure.

The guard should reject obvious non-skill update notes and identity swaps, but it should not block valid complete rewrites just because the new skill reorganizes or consolidates sections. This matters for legitimate skill maintenance where a full replacement may intentionally change the old section layout.

## What changed

- Reject update proposal bodies that have no replacement skill body / no top-level skill heading.
- Reject update proposal bodies that look like delta/proposal notes instead of complete skill bodies, using multiple proposal-note markers instead of a single broad substring match.
- Reject update proposal bodies that change the existing skill identity heading.
- Allow complete replacement `SKILL.md` bodies that intentionally reorganize or consolidate old `##` sections.
- Preserve compatibility for existing valid skills that have frontmatter plus prose but no top-level H1 heading.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - delta/proposal-note update drafts;
  - empty replacement bodies;
  - complete section rewrites;
  - single proposal-like section headings inside an otherwise complete skill;
  - emoji-decorated preserved headings.

## Duplicate/adjacent work check

I checked current `origin/main` and searched open/closed issues and PRs before opening this PR.

Not duplicates:

- #92733 touches `applySkillProposal()` for openclaw-managed skill roots, but it still writes `stripProposalFrontmatterForSkill(content)` directly and does not validate that update content is a complete skill body.
- #94315 / #94249 cover large-proposal apply timeout behavior.
- #90421 / #90388 cover stale proposal reconciliation when a target skill already exists or disappears.
- #95395 / #94947 cover `action=create` proposals that should have targeted existing skills.

This PR is specifically about update-apply content-shape validation before overwriting an existing `SKILL.md`.

## Evidence

Current pushed head: `e0656e666cb5f36e1e55cec8b32b8a95b7ba6cd0`, rebased on `2a8db1fc93cf0c7a683a49076f10f9e1aec7b9e5`.

Exact validation run after the latest fix:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Observed results on the exact pushed head:

```text
src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts: 30 passed
tsgo core-test: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Real apply-time proof outside the unit test shard:

```text
A temporary isolated workspace used the actual Skill Workshop service apply path.

bad delta-note update -> rejected
original SKILL.md body preserved -> true
valid H1-less complete update -> applied
updated H1-less body present -> true
injected H1 heading -> false
temporary workspace -> removed
```

Redacted terminal output from that proof:

```json
{
  "event": "delta-note-apply",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "rejected": true,
  "rejection": "Skill update proposal content appears to be a delta/proposal note, not a complete replacement SKILL.md. Revise the proposal with the full updated skill body before applying.",
  "originalBodyPreserved": true,
  "proposalStillPending": true
}
{
  "event": "h1-less-complete-update",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "applied": true,
  "updatedBodyPresent": true,
  "hasInjectedH1": false
}
{
  "event": "cleanup",
  "removed": "<temp-workspace-root>"
}
```

Additional behavior covered by the focused test shard:

```text
empty replacement body -> rejected with the existing SKILL.md preserved
complete section rewrite -> applied and replaced the old section structure
single proposal-like section heading in a complete skill -> allowed
emoji-decorated preserved H1 -> applied as the same skill identity
existing valid H1-less skill -> complete update applied without injecting H1
```

What was not tested:

- Full repository CI is expected to run on GitHub for the pushed head.

commands.author: simplyclever914

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add session history family lookup
target.body:
## Problem

When a threaded conversation has already been split across reset transcripts, `chat.history` and the `sessions_history` tool only read the currently active transcript. The session store can already retain the family through `usageFamilySessionIds`, but history readers do not use that chain and do not discover archived `*.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` topic transcripts.

That means an agent/tool can be looking at the correct logical Telegram/Slack/Discord thread and still miss earlier turns after session rollover.

## Fix

Add an opt-in family history path:

- `chat.history` accepts `includeFamily: true`.
- `sessions_history` accepts `includeFamily`.
- Gateway and embedded `chat.history` now share the bounded/header-checked reset archive resolver instead of using separate prefix scans.
- The shared resolver can include owned topic-suffixed reset archives such as `<sessionId>-topic-456.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` only when the archive header matches the requested session id.
- Same-prefix colliding topic archives and headerless topic archives are ignored.
- Family read targets are capped before transcript reads, and normal `chat.history` behavior remains opt-in: UI startup does not load ancestor history.

## Validation

Current head: `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`.

- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts`
  - gateway shard: 84 tests passed
  - agents shard: 11 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
  - 36 tests passed
- `CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
  - `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed, no output

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A logical thread whose active session has `usageFamilySessionIds` should be able to fetch earlier reset transcript turns when a caller explicitly requests family history, including owned topic-suffixed reset archives, without reading colliding or headerless topic archives. The current active transcript must also be preserved when a retained reset family has more entries than the bounded 32 read targets.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at current head `a12e1bbcf0`, using temporary on-disk session directories, the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC integration harness, embedded-mode `chat.history`, and the same reset archive filename/header patterns that OpenClaw creates for topic transcripts.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created temporary session directories and session-store entries with `usageFamilySessionIds` linking an ancestor session and the current session.
  2. Wrote an owned plain ancestor reset archive and an owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive with matching `type: "session"` headers.
  3. Wrote newer same-prefix colliding and headerless topic reset archives in the same sessions directory.
  4. Invoked the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC path with and without `includeFamily: true`.
  5. Created a second Gateway/RPC case with 40 ancestor session ids before the current id in `usageFamilySessionIds`.
  6. Invoked embedded-mode `chat.history(includeFamily)` through the sessions tool stub, including the same >32-family-target cap case.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from current head:
  ```text
  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  84 passed (84)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.69s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Default `chat.history` excludes ancestor reset archive content, while `chat.history(includeFamily)` includes the owned plain ancestor archive, includes the owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive, includes current-session content, and rejects the colliding/headerless topic reset archives. In the >32-target Gateway/RPC regression, `chat.history(includeFamily)` contains `current survives family target cap`, contains early ancestor content, and does not read the ancestor entries beyond the target cap. Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now reads the current active transcript first, then current archives, then ancestor archives/active transcripts; its >32-target regression also keeps the current transcript and drops over-cap ancestors.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram topic end-to-end, and this PR does not add prompt-level carryover summaries or automatic prompt injection. It only adds explicit family history retrieval for gateway/tool callers.

commands.author: anyech

target.title: fix(agents): verify completion delivery target
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens ACP/subagent completion announcement delivery accounting so a completion is only counted as visibly delivered when the delivery evidence matches the requester route.

It:
- propagates outbound payload target metadata into delivery status
- validates automatic completion delivery against provider/account/channel/thread targets
- requires exact account/thread evidence for threaded origins
- preserves target metadata on failed payload outcomes for diagnostics
- keeps the current-chat generated-media compatibility path while avoiding explicit wrong-target matches

## Behavior addressed

Completion announcements can be injected through an internal/inter-session path while the original requester is a channel/thread. Broad checks such as “visible payload exists” or “message tool sent something” can count delivery as successful even when the visible delivery evidence belongs to another route.

This change makes completion delivery proof target-aware: wrong thread, missing thread, missing account, and provider mismatches no longer satisfy delivery for a concrete requester route.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Completion delivery is only counted as visibly delivered when runtime delivery evidence matches the requester route, including concrete account/thread target. Wrong-thread message-tool evidence must be rejected; exact-thread evidence must be accepted.
- **Real setup tested**: Disposable checkout of PR branch `anyech/openclaw:fix/acp-completion-thread-delivery` under `tmp/github-open-work/real-proof-20260603T073207Z/pr-89287`. A one-off non-production runtime harness (`proof-89287.ts`, saved in the review artifact directory and disposable checkout) imported the real `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` module and called `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` through the real announce dispatch/direct-delivery path. Synthetic session store/config/origin data used a fake Discord group/thread route; only the boundary gateway/transport deps were substituted to record and return delivery envelopes. No production Gateway/config/runtime/state or private deployment proof was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Terminal commands run in the disposable checkout were `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts` and `node --import tsx proof-89287.ts`; full command transcript is in `review/real-proof-build-20260603T073207Z/messaging/exact-commands-used.txt`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output / runtime log excerpt from `review/real-proof-build-20260603T073207Z/messaging/evidence/proof-89287.log`:

  ```json
  {
    "agentParams": {"deliver":false,"channel":"discord","to":"channel:proof-parent","threadId":"thread-proof-expected","sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"},
    "wrong": {"delivered":false,"path":"direct","reason":"message_tool_delivery_missing","error":"completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"},
    "correct": {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: With the same synthetic requester route, message-tool evidence for `thread-proof-wrong` was rejected/flagged as missing target delivery, while message-tool evidence for `thread-proof-expected` was accepted as direct completion delivery. This exercised the repository runtime announce/direct-delivery implementation, not only an isolated matcher function.
- **What was not tested**: No live production Gateway, live Discord workspace, real bot token, or private deployment state was used. The outbound provider and gateway agent call were substituted only at the non-production boundary so the proof remains sanitized.

commands.author: NarahariRaghava

target.title: fix(config): allow wildcard * as a valid channel id in channel config validation
target.body:
## Summary
- Config validation was rejecting `channels["*"]` with "unknown channel id: *"
- The `*` wildcard is a valid special case meaning "apply to all channels"
- This blocked users from configuring wildcard channel settings like `channels["*"].ackReaction`
- Fix: add `"*"` to the `allowedChannels` set in `src/config/validation.ts`
- This is a follow-up to #91857 which fixes the runtime behavior — this PR unblocks live proof for that fix

## Linked context
Closes # N/A
Related # 91857
Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — discovered as a blocker while raising #91857

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: `channels["*"].ackReaction` config rejected at startup with "unknown channel id: *"
- Real environment tested: macOS, Ollama/llama3.2, ClickClack channel
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: added `channels["*"].ackReaction = "🎯"` to openclaw.json — OpenClaw now starts successfully without validation error
- Evidence after fix: 57/57 unit tests passing including 2 new tests specifically for wildcard validation
- Observed result after fix: config validation accepts `"*"` as a valid channel id
- What was not tested: all possible wildcard config combinations
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none for this fix — validation change is directly testable

## Tests and validation
Commands run: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Results: 57/57 tests passing

Regression coverage added:
- "accepts wildcard * as a valid channel id in channels config"
- "still rejects unknown non-wildcard channel ids"

What failed before this fix: OpenClaw refused to start with `channels["*"]` in config

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — config validation now accepts `"*"` as a channel id
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes — previously invalid config is now valid
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
What is the highest-risk area? Accepting a new config shape that was previously rejected
How is that risk mitigated? `"*"` is explicitly allowlisted as a special case — random unknown channel ids still fail validation. New test confirms invalid ids are still rejected.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review.
This PR and #91857 are related — together they fully fix wildcard channel config support.
target.author: narahariraghava
